data.name: riskAssessmentNotes.Text
data.value: The suitability of all materials used in manufacturing should be taken from conformity reports. Where not available these should be requested from the manufacturer/importer. Safety aspects should be taken from the user manual provided by the manufacturer. If not provided then this should be requested or created by the controller. Not to be used in wind speeds in excess of 38Kph. A competent adult should supervise the inflatable and users at all times. All anchor points to be used at all times.

data.name: label100.Text
data.value: If you need to take some additional photos, you can do that here! They will add as an additional page on the report. Good for taking pictures of the pressure reading and areas that you might want to explain on the risk assessment in more detail.

data.name: creditsText.Text
data.value:
While writing this applcation I asked a lot of people for advice and help - It's only fair that I mention them here (in no order of relevance).
Nigel Davis - Technical Director at the RPII provided steer, guidance, documentation and feedback on various inspection wording and advice on the standard throughout the entire development: https://www.playinspectors.com/ Stefan Burke - Who I collaborated with on various elements of coding; Check him out for software development and all things technical: https://chobble.com/ Chris Winters - Inflatable Inspector who provided guidance specifically on the toddler zone and helped develop my understanding; Inflatable inspections and repairs in Birmingham: https://www.jumpinjacks.net/category/repairs-and-testing Paul Quaif - Inflatable Inspector who helped form my understanding of inspecting in general and supported me while I performed my inspector training: https://www.playinspectors.com/inspector/?i=6408 Mike Kerr - Inflatable Inspector who helped form my understanding of catchbed requirements and how to inspect them, providing input on what must be captured and is relevant: https://www.dmkleisure.co.uk/ John Quaif - Inflatable Inspector who provided insight into how inspectors operate in the field and helped navigate how a useful UI should operate for an inspector: https://www.playinspectors.com/inspector/?i=6359
Various open-source libraries used: PDFsharp, SQLite and RestSharp https://www.pdfsharp.com https://restsharp.dev/ https://system.data.sqlite.org/

data.name: label16.Text
data.value:
User counts only need to be calculated for the user height that applies - If a unit doesn't allow users over 1m tall, then do not calculate the counts for these users as it doesn't apply. 1 meter tall user count = Playing area / 1 1.2 meter tall user count = Playing area / 1.33 1.5 meter tall user count = Playing area / 1.66 1.8 meter tall user count = Playing area / 2
The playing area doesn't include steps and if obstacles and biff bash are present then you need to reduce this area. Judgement can be used here, but must be cautious considering over-loading and evacuation times do factor into this!
